qa_pairs.question: As of July 31, 2025, what is the status of the computational nodes listed for the Beluga cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: As of July 31, 2025, all computational nodes listed for the Beluga cluster are turned off. Turbo mode is activated on all Beluga nodes.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the Beluga nodes equipped with GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: 172 Beluga nodes feature 40 cores, 186GB (or 191000M) of available memory, 2 x Intel Gold 6148 Skylake @ 2.4 GHz CPUs, 1 x 1.6TB NVMe SSD for storage, and 4 x NVidia V100SXM2 GPUs (16GB memory) connected via NVLink.

qa_pairs.question: How can users monitor their computational tasks on the Beluga cluster, and what information can they visualize?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can monitor their CPU and GPU computational tasks in 'real-time' or review past tasks via the Beluga portal (https://portail.beluga.calculquebec.ca/). This allows them to visualize CPU core utilization, memory utilization, and GPU utilization, which helps maximize resource use and reduce wait times.
